primary_factors: Morning precip is primarily heavy rain with temps just above freezing (≈33–35°F) and wind chills well above extreme-cold closure thresholds; no credible ≤-20°F wind chill signal
Main AM hazards are ponding/standing water + reduced visibility + patchy bridge/overpass slickness (localized), not widespread plowable snow/ice
Wintry/accumulating wet snow and stronger winds are most likely AFTER noon (more relevant to early release/PM travel than canceling the entire day)
No confirmed neighboring-district closures or Rockford delay/closure messaging found; community sentiment appears uncertain (no strong “domino effect”)
Power outages currently not a major driver (very low statewide; Kent-specific counts inconsistent but no indication of widespread outages impacting schools)
decision_rationale:
Integrating the six expert inputs, the consensus outcome is that a FULL snow day (closure before the school day starts) is unlikely for Rockford on Wed 3/11 because the commute window is dominated by heavy rain—not heavy snow—and temperatures are marginally above freezing. The web verifier did not find any credible ≤-20°F wind chill (and Weather.com hourly could not be accessed, but no other source suggested dangerous cold), so the extreme-cold automatic closure rules are not triggered. 

Where risk does exist is operational: very high rainfall/ponding plus near-freezing conditions can create localized slick bridges/overpasses and reduced visibility/hydroplaning risk during the 6–8 AM bus window. Infrastructure guidance aligns: highways likely wet/driveable; hazards are patchy rather than countywide impassable. Historically, these March warm-advection rain-to-snow setups in West Michigan produce delays more often than closures unless an unexpected colder pre-dawn window creates a more uniform glaze or there are confirmed road closures/flooding on key bus routes. 

News intelligence found no confirmed Rockford or neighboring district closures/delays and no Kent County-specific confirmed road-closure reports in the scan; that removes the “domino effect” that often pushes borderline calls into closure territory. Power-grid conditions are not currently forcing a closure.

Recommendation basis: OPEN with strong monitoring and contingency planning for a 2-hour delay if pre-dawn temps/pavement temps dip to freezing or if route checks find multiple unsafe segments (standing water across roads, bridge icing, or multiple bus-route problem spots).

collaboration.summary: Agents reached consensus after 1 round(s) with 17.8% spread. Average probability: 14%.
